incidents.title: DB connection pool exhausted
incidents.description: A Helm values change shrinks checkout-service's DB connection pool from 50 to 10, causing a pool-exhausted alert ~90s later. A decoy deploy (unrelated logging-library bump) happened 31 minutes earlier and must be ranked below the real cause.

rca_candidates.summary: reduce db.connectionPoolSize 50 -> 10
bump logging library version

incidents.title: Pod crash loop detected: payments-service restarting repeatedly
incidents.description: A deploy changes payments-service's container entrypoint to a script that doesn't exist in the image, sending pods into CrashLoopBackOff ~3 minutes later. Two decoys: an unrelated same-service deploy 40 minutes earlier, and a ledger-service deploy that lands *closer* to the alert than the culprit — time proximity alone would rank the wrong deploy first.

alerts.title: Pod crash loop detected: payments-service restarting repeatedly

rca_candidates.summary: change container entrypoint to ./run.sh and drop wrapper command
add monthly settlement report
update receipt email copy

incidents.title: Pod OOMKilled: memory usage exceeded limit
incidents.description: A Helm values change halves inventory-service's memory limit. Memory climbs for 90 minutes before pods start getting OOMKilled. A decoy deploy (tracing instrumentation) lands just 10 minutes before the alert — time proximity favors the decoy, and diff keyword evidence must outweigh it.

alerts.title: Pod OOMKilled: memory usage exceeded limit

rca_candidates.summary: reduce container memory limit 512Mi to 256Mi in resources block
add tracing spans to stock lookup

incidents.title: Deployment replicas unavailable: image pull failure
incidents.description: A deploy bumps notifications-service to image tag v2.4.1, which was never pushed to the registry (should have been v2.4.0); pods immediately fail to pull. The decoy is a same-service registry-credentials rotation 20 minutes earlier whose diff partially matches the alert's keywords (registry) — both candidates fire diff_keyword_match, so the ranking must discriminate on keyword strength and timing, not rule presence.

alerts.title: Deployment replicas unavailable: image pull failure

rca_candidates.summary: bump image tag to v2.4.1
rotate container registry credentials

incidents.title: checkout-service: payment request timeouts spiking
incidents.description: A ConfigMap change (via ArgoCD sync) sets payments-service's HTTP client timeout to 50ms instead of 5000ms. Payments' outbound calls start timing out, and the alert fires on checkout-service — one dependency hop away from the true cause. The decoy is a same-service checkout deploy 20 minutes before the alert. A pipeline that can't traverse the graph in the causal direction ranks the decoy first.

rca_candidates.summary: set HTTP_CLIENT_TIMEOUT_MS to 50 in payments ConfigMap
refactor cart session handling

incidents.title: api-gateway: 401 spike after token secret validation failures
incidents.description: auth-service rotates its JWT signing secret, but tokens issued under the old key are still in flight and the gateway's cached keyset no longer validates them — 401s spike at api-gateway, two dependency hops from the cause. The decoy (a user-service query optimization) sits only one hop away, so graph distance favors the decoy; auth's blast radius as a shared dependency plus the secret keyword evidence must overcome it.

alerts.title: api-gateway: 401 spike after token secret validation failures

rca_candidates.summary: rotate JWT signing secret for auth-service
optimize profile query caching

incidents.title: checkout-service: 500 error rate spike on /cart/discount
incidents.description: checkout-service ships three deploys in one afternoon with no canary. The middle one reworks discount-code validation and silently rejects legacy code formats; nothing fails until a marketing campaign (SUMMER26) drives traffic into the broken path 105 minutes later. All three candidates tie on time-window presence, ownership, and blast radius, and the newest deploy is closest to the alert — only the 'discount'/'cart' tokens from the alert title link the diff to the failure.

rca_candidates.summary: rework discount code validation in cart flow
adjust logging verbosity for payment client
update SEO metadata rendering

incidents.title: orders-service: database deadlock errors spiking
incidents.description: billing-service ships an invoice-reconciliation job that batch-updates the orders table; its lock ordering collides with orders-service's row updates and deadlocks spike 4 minutes later. There is no depends_on path between the two services in either direction — they are coupled only as co-writers of orders-db. The decoy is a same-service orders-service deploy 45 minutes before the alert; a graph that can't see sibling coupling ranks the decoy first.

rca_candidates.summary: add invoice reconciliation job that batch-updates orders table in a transaction
improve order search pagination

incidents.title: orders-service: p99 latency high on order history queries
incidents.description: An orders-service 'cleanup' migration drops an index believed unused; order-history queries fall back to sequential scans and p99 climbs for 70 minutes. The decoy — a billing-service report job reading the same orders table — is sibling-coupled via orders-db, keyword-adjacent, and lands 15 minutes before the alert: it fires all four rules and must still lose to the same-service culprit with the stronger diff match.

alerts.title: orders-service: p99 latency high on order history queries

rca_candidates.summary: drop unused index idx_orders_customer_created on orders table
add hourly revenue report job reading orders table

incidents.title: payments-service: HTTP 500 error rate critical
incidents.description: payments-service enables strict TLS verification with a broken CA bundle; every outbound gateway call fails. Within four minutes, alerts fire on payments, checkout (1 hop), refunds (1 hop), and api-gateway (2 hops). The decoy — an unrelated checkout deploy 10 minutes before the culprit — scores well against the checkout alert alone but is causally invisible to the payments alert; averaging across the storm separates a cause that explains everything from one that explains one thing.

rca_candidates.summary: enable strict TLS verification for outbound payment gateway connections

historical_pattern_match.past_root_cause: {'files_changed': ['configmap.yaml'], 'summary': 'set HTTP_CLIENT_TIMEOUT_MS to 50 in payments ConfigMap'} HTTP_CLIENT_TIMEOUT_MS set to 50 (unit confusion, meant 5000); payments gateway calls time out, surfacing as checkout payment failures.

rca_candidates.summary: update checkout page banner copy

incidents.title: uploads-service: S3 access denied (AccessDenied) on object downloads
incidents.description: A least-privilege cleanup removes s3:GetObject from uploads-service's IAM role. Nothing fails until the service's cached credentials refresh 50 minutes later, when downloads start returning AccessDenied. A same-service code deploy (thumbnail generation) lands 10 minutes before the alert — time proximity favors it; the access/object keyword evidence must pull the Terraform change back to rank one.

alerts.title: uploads-service: S3 access denied (AccessDenied) on object downloads

rca_candidates.summary: remove s3:GetObject and s3:ListBucket access from uploads-service role
add thumbnail generation for image previews

incidents.title: payments-service: database connection timeouts spiking
incidents.description: Someone tightens payments-db's security group in the AWS console; the drift detector captures the delta. Five-minute-old connections start timing out and payments-service alerts — one hop downstream of the drifted resource. The true cause has no git SHA, no changed files, and no commit in evidence; the decoy (a same-service email-template deploy) has all three and must still lose.

rca_candidates.summary: drift detected: security group ingress rule removed — postgres 5432 database access from payments subnet
restructure invoice email templates

incidents.title: checkout-service: upstream hostname resolution failures (DNS)
incidents.description: A CoreDNS Corefile change replaces the upstream resolver list and internal hostname resolution starts failing. The alert fires on checkout-service, one hop from cluster-dns — a node three services depend on. The decoy (a checkout hero-image deploy) is same-service and five minutes from the alert; the shared-infra culprit wins on the dns/resolver keyword class, its 3-dependent blast radius, and graph coupling.

rca_candidates.summary: tighten CoreDNS forward plugin: replace upstream resolver list
update product page hero images

incidents.title: pricing-service: pricing calculation errors spiking
incidents.description: The new-pricing-engine flag ramps 0% -> 100% and pricing errors spike three minutes later: negative totals on bundle SKUs. The flag flip has no commit, no files, no rollout — the same-service decoy (a currency refactor deployed 45 minutes earlier) has all the code artifacts and must still lose to a console click.

rca_candidates.summary: ramp flag new-pricing-engine 0% -> 100% for pricing-service
refactor currency conversion helpers

incidents.title: search-service: metrics absent — scrape target down
incidents.description: A deploy renames search-service's metrics port (9090 -> 9464) and moves the handler; Prometheus scrapes hit connection refused and the absent-target alert fires four minutes later. The service itself is fully healthy — no errors, no k8s events — the only casualty is observability. The decoy is an earlier same-service ranking change with no telemetry keywords.

alerts.title: search-service: metrics absent — scrape target down

rca_candidates.summary: rename metrics port 9090 to 9464 and move handler to /telemetry

historical_pattern_match.past_root_cause: {'files_changed': ['prometheus/scrape_configs.yaml'], 'summary': 'consolidate scrape configs: rewrite job relabeling for service targets'} rewritten relabeling rules drop all service targets; every scrape fails and absent-metrics alerts cascade across monitored services.

rca_candidates.summary: tune ranking boost weights

incidents.title: checkout-service: metrics absent — scrape target down
incidents.description: A scrape-config consolidation rewrites job relabeling and drops every service target; absent-metrics alerts fire on checkout, payments, and orders within two minutes. All three services are healthy and none depends on Prometheus at runtime — the culprit is reachable only through monitored_by edges, gated to observability-shaped symptoms. The decoy (a checkout gift-card deploy) explains one alert of three; storm aggregation buries it.

alerts.title: checkout-service: metrics absent — scrape target down
payments-service: metrics absent — scrape target down
orders-service: metrics absent — scrape target down

rca_candidates.summary: consolidate scrape configs: rewrite job relabeling for service targets

historical_pattern_match.past_root_cause: {'files_changed': ['telemetry/server.py', 'deployment.yaml'], 'summary': 'rename metrics port 9090 to 9464 and move handler to /telemetry'} scrape config still targets 9090/metrics; the handler now lives on 9464/telemetry, so every scrape fails.

rca_candidates.summary: add gift card support to cart
